# Procedural Pretraining: Warming Up Language Models with Abstract Data

* Authors: EPFL/Idiap/Adelaide
* Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.21725
* Date: 2026-01-29

## Notes

* model is initially exposed to abstract structured data, e.g. procedural data, generated by formal languages and other
  simple algorithms
* on context recall (NEEDLE-IN-A-HAYSTACK), the accuracy jumps from 10 to 98% when pretraining on Dyck sequences (
  balanced brackets)
* front-loading as little as 0.1% procedural data significantly outperforms standard pretraining on natural language,
  code, and informal mathematics (C4, CODEPARROT, and DEEPMIND-MATH datasets)
* procedural pretraining enables the models to reach the same loss value with only 55 / 67 / 86% of the original data
* procedural pretraining instills non-trivial structure in both attention and MLP layer
* LLMs simultaneously acquire multiple forms of knowledge during pretraining, absorb rich semantic content, but also
  acquire abilities for manipulating this knowledge. This entangled learning of knowledge and abstract skills has been
  identified as a key limitation of current models, leading to their reliance on surface-level heuristics rather than
  systematic reasoning procedures

## Ideas

* can you pre-pre-train without synthetic data?
